title: prunus subcordata
intro: Prunus subcordata, also known as the Oregon plum, is a deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The Oregon plum has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Prunus subcordata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Herbal preparations of the Oregon plum include teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be made from the bark, leaves, or fruit of the plant.
